title: TreeLens tree node
description: A pure-structure hierarchy node (wire-protocol.md §3). Name/visible/opacity/bounds/effects live in attrs, NOT here. The root is a sentinel: id=null, type="ROOT".

id.description: Stable node id, unique within the scope. MUST survive undo/redo. Matching is strictly by id (no positional fallback).

type.description: Host-defined node kind (e.g. GROUP, PIXEL, TEXT). A container/group is a normal node with non-empty children.

rootSentinel.description: The tree root. id MUST be null and type MUST be "ROOT" — a fixed protocol marker, NOT a host name: the adapter normalizes its host root (e.g. Photoshop's "DOCUMENT") to "ROOT". The kernel is type-agnostic, but the hash includes type, so the marker is fixed for cross-language hash determinism (wire-protocol.md §3). Top-level nodes are its children; the sentinel itself is never surfaced to the model.
